chunk_summary: This section discusses affordable and practical ways to ensure adequate protein intake in childhood, emphasizing the importance of protein quality and quantity through dietary sources like dal and curd, supported by authoritative guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of protein in childhood growth and development.
Learn affordable dietary strategies to meet protein requirements in children.
Evaluate protein quality using established guidelines and recommendations.
Explore the importance of protein supplementation versus natural food sources.
key_facts: Adding one katori of dal at lunch and curd at dinner provides sufficient protein for children.
Protein quality evaluation is guided by FAO's 2013 recommendations on human nutrition.
ICMR-NIN's 2020 guidelines emphasize natural food sources over supplements for safety and cost-effectiveness.
Enteral nutrition guidelines from ESPGHAN (2022) are particularly relevant for preterm infants.
Dietary guidelines for Indians (ICMR-NIN, 2024) provide updated recommendations for protein intake across age groups.
reference_quote: Let us add one katori of dal at lunch and curd at dinner; this is cheaper and safer than supplements.
document_key_topics: Protein quantity and quality in childhood nutrition
Affordable dietary strategies for protein intake
Guidelines for protein evaluation and supplementation
